item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this take on AWCY. Not everything has to be art that makes you go crazy or kills you/someone else!

Same with the gold urinal right next to this one on the list!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"It'd be weirder if it was something insanely irregular like 17:11 or e:pi."

DO NOT TEMPT ME, MORTAL.
DO NOT TEMPT ME, MORTAL.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hell yeah, demoscene skip! Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so incredibly clever and fresh, yet people don't appreciate it. Shame on them.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Using a ratio like that implies a composition that features polyrhythm throughout, which allows for permanent rhythmic conflict and contradictory accents, perhaps in a way that is only understandable through Gestalt perception?

Anyways 7:4 in 4/4 time would be seven quarter notes that are spaced so as to have the duration of 4 quarter notes. Though it's probably not obvious enough to be a joke? It'd be weirder if it was something insanely irregular like 17:11 or e:pi.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meh. I found the whole crack intro and DMCA thing somewhat funny, but not really. No vote.

Also, just nitpicking here, but shouldn't 7:4 be 7/4? I don't know too much about music theory, but I think that's how you write it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha, this was actually pretty cool. It took me until the sample log to make the connection, but then I laughed. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation received an interdimensional DMCA notice. Heh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The synth nerd in me says "upvote."

Gotta ask though, how are they confirmed countefeited? Also. I can't help but think of that scene from Zoolander… Relax, don't do it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: a) Or maybe he has a crystal ball that can see into the Alexylvaverse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems rather pointless to have obviously counterfeit pieces of art when you can get reproductions anywhere.

Yes. I'd love it if this article made these more than just copies in an interesting way, though I'm out of ideas about what that could be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's exactly my concern. I'm starting to really like the SCPs that seem inhuman and completely alien. There's just an annoyingly large number of quirky ones that like to screw with the foundation in silly ways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was this >< close to 'cracking' the actual skip with my upvote and comment, but I'm sure it'd have been reverted hard for being too meta even though I'd have made sure Randomini was on board with it. Oh well! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Witchcraft!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will find a way to upvote this comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a) "SPECIAL THANKS TO BRIAN_29 FOR THE ORIGINAL SOURCE"; i.e. Someone from the Alexylverse (BRIAN_29) provided it to AWCY to "crack".
b) Alexylva U's art department could stick antipiracy tracking stuff in their creative works, in the same way some cracked software still reports back to a server when they're installed or running. It's probably memetic or something.
c) If they've got tracking stuff, they'd have noticed a big group of "cracked" artwork at Site-39.
d) Who receives more cease-and-desist notices, cracking groups or torrent sites? The torrent sites do, because they're the ones hosting it. Similarly, the Foundation's the one "exhibiting" the cracked artworks, so they're the ones who would receive the cease-and-desist.

…not that I know anything about torrenting cracked software or anything.
._.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dig reality cracking, and AWCY seem like the group to do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I immediately thought of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is me as well. Classic works, forged or not, combined with the cracktros I've seen for most of my life, equals too much cheese to consume.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The carrier pigeons were getting shot down too quickly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love Crack intros!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted, but the last bit… sigh. I'm really tired of EVERYONE and their mother knowing how to anonymously mail the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I missed the interesting part?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just upvoted awcy

~going home in defeat~

item.title: (no title)
(no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1617 CONTAINMENT PROCEDURES HACKED BY *PIMP_MASTA*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That actually doesn't bother me, per se. AWCY is presumably a pretty big group, and some subgroups within it will make more effort or behave differently than others. Furthermore, not all of them are going to be doing huge, world-fucking projects. This one works for me.

Now, my main issue is that I'm not sure what you're doing with Alexylva here. I love the idea of different users bringing their own headcanon into it, but do you have some idea of a) how AWCY got access to a Universiverse piece of artwork, b) how the Universiverse's equivalent of the Patents and Trademarks Office heard of it, c) how they sent a letter to the Foundation site where it was being held, and d) why they care about the Foundation's role in containing it? More specifically, why is the Foundation getting the cease-and-desist rather than AWCY?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I used to have a lot of cracked computer games when I was a kid, so I found that pretty amusing (it was not uncommon to keep a shitty game just because it had a great crack intro). But overall it felt a bit simplistic and didn't take the idea very far. It seems rather pointless to have obviously counterfeit pieces of art when you can get reproductions anywhere. I think this idea could be more effectively used for something else, but I don't have suggestions right now. Neutral vote for me.

are not known to have existed in any form.

This seems to imply that they are not from this world, when there are millions of art pieces in existence that nobody heard about except those who made them. I would just say something like ''previously unknown'' and let the reader use their imagination.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing about the writing by itself bothers me here, but what does is what it says about the GoI in question, or rather what it doesn't. It's just the same AWCY article we've seen far too many times for my taste, with them being all subversive to traditional art, throwing an anomalous effect with a message to the mix, and calling it a day. It just doesn't work for me at this point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is clever, especially considering our current and lasting copyright focus. And its also good in that its the take on AWCY that they aren't crazy, evil people…they just want to make people happy and let them enjoy art…well, their brand or anart, anyway. I like it +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you don't understand, http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crack_intro

Thanks to MrCarbon for the idea and feedback!
